title: The importance of meat and organs during pregnancy and preconception

chunks.title: Introduction to Animal-Based Diets
chunks.summary: The discussion begins with an introduction to the advocate for animal-based diets and their significance in women's health, particularly during pregnancy and postpartum. The speaker reflects on their educational background and the lack of emphasis on animal-based nutrition in traditional training.

chunks.summary: The speaker emphasizes the importance of nutrient-dense foods, particularly organ meats, for pregnant women. They argue that traditional dietary recommendations often overlook these crucial foods and provide anecdotal evidence of their benefits from clients.

chunks.title: Optimal Prenatal Nutrition
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts to optimal prenatal nutrition, focusing on the necessity of specific nutrients like folate and riboflavin. The speakers discuss the best dietary sources for these nutrients, primarily found in egg yolks and liver, and stress the disadvantages of relying on leafy greens.

chunks.title: Concerns with Leafy Greens
chunks.summary: The speaker elaborates on the negative components of leafy greens that can hinder nutrient absorption and affect thyroid function, making them less favorable compared to animal-based foods. They highlight specific compounds present in vegetables that may cause issues during pregnancy.

chunks.summary: Discussions continue on the role of organ meats in prenatal vitamins, with the speaker outlining their ideal composition for prenatal nutrition. Emphasis is placed on the importance of liver, heart, and potentially spleen for pregnant women to ensure optimal nutrient intake.

chunks.title: Animal Fats in Pregnancy
chunks.summary: The conversation concludes with an acknowledgment of the importance of animal fats, such as tallow and grass-fed butter, especially for pregnant women. The speakers discuss various options to incorporate these healthy fats to support overall health during pregnancy.
